title: Economics Foundations
description: This course introduces **the fundamental principles of economics**, explaining how individuals, businesses, and governments make decisions under conditions of scarcity.

title: Microeconomics
description: This course studies **how individuals, households, and firms make decisions** and how these decisions determine prices, production, and resource allocation in markets.

title: Macroeconomics
description: This course studies **the economy as a whole**, including national income, inflation, unemployment, economic growth, and the policies governments and central banks use to stabilize the economy.

title: Consumer Theory
description: This course studies **how consumers make decisions** under limited income, explaining how preferences, prices, and budgets determine demand for goods and services.

title: Producer Theory
description: This course studies **how firms make production decisions**, combining labor, capital, and technology to produce goods and services while minimizing costs and maximizing profits.

title: Market Structures
description: This course studies **how different types of markets influence competition, pricing, production, and consumer welfare**. It examines how firms behave under different competitive conditions.

title: International Economics
description: This course studies **how countries trade, invest, and interact economically**. It explains the principles of international trade, exchange rates, trade policies, and the global financial system.

title: Public Economics
description: This course studies **the role of government in the economy**, including taxation, public spending, regulation, and policies designed to improve economic efficiency and social welfare.

title: Monetary Economics
description: This course studies **money, banking, financial systems, and monetary policy**. It explains how money is created, how central banks influence the economy, and how financial stability is maintained.

title: Development Economics
description: This course studies **how economies grow and develop over time**, focusing on poverty reduction, inequality, industrialization, human development, and sustainable economic progress.

title: Behavioral Economics
description: This course studies **how psychology influences economic decision-making**. It explains why people often make choices that differ from the predictions of traditional economics.

title: Econometrics
description: This course studies **how statistical and mathematical methods are used to analyze economic data**, test economic theories, estimate relationships, and make forecasts.
